alerts.title: Iran targets US forces in Kuwait; US retaliates with strikes on Iranian radar
alerts.summary: US military reports Iran launched multiple drones and missiles at American positions in Kuwait on June 4-5, with US forces conducting counter-strikes on Iranian radar installations per incidents feed (BBC, The Hill). Event represents active kinetic escalation between US and Iran forces, distinct from proxy activity. Occurs amid ongoing ceasefire negotiations and elevated threat level (9/10 CRITICAL).

alerts.title: Hezbollah rejects ceasefire; resumes extensive rocket, drone attacks on northern Israel
alerts.summary: Despite June 3 US-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire agreement, Hezbollah launched sustained rocket and drone strikes on Kiryat Shmona, Nahariyya, Shlomi, and Mediterranean positions per Telegram (ME Spectator, ~50K-66K views across posts). New development shows ceasefire collapse within 24-48 hours, marking material escalation cycle. FPV drones targeted IDF command vehicles, narrowly missing Major General Rafi Milo two weeks prior.

alerts.title: Iran exploits Iraq's Umm Qasr port to circumvent US economic blockade
alerts.summary: Iran reportedly establishing Umm Qasr (southern Iraq) as logistics hub to bypass US sanctions and blockade per incidents feed (Iran International). Development represents economic escalation and potential sanction-evasion network activation amid active hostilities. Suggests preparation for sustained conflict operations and Iranian supply-chain resilience.

alerts.title: Houthi-Al-Shabaab coordination threatens Red Sea shipping chokepoint
alerts.summary: Asia Times and CFR reports indicate Houthis and Al-Shabaab terrorist organization are coordinating to disrupt Red Sea maritime routes per incidents feed. Multiple Red Sea shipping attack warnings from AP News, BBC corroborate Houthi escalation. Development signals proxy coordination across Indian Ocean-Red Sea axis and threat to global maritime commerce (Brent crude at $95.1 per global_metrics).

alerts.title: US domestic political polarization surges amid Iran crisis; Trump dominates discourse
alerts.summary: Sentiment analysis shows near-parity negative/positive (46% neg vs 45% pos) in US news with 'Trump' appearing 68 times in top trending terms, followed by 'senate', 'iran', 'republicans', 'putin'. Word frequency spike on partisan actors (Trump, Senate, Republicans, Graham, Biden) indicates domestic political divisions overshadowing unified Iran response. Strategic concern: fractured US domestic consensus weakens negotiating position amid active conflict.

analysis_note: Threat level remains CRITICAL (9/10) with concurrent kinetic escalation between US-Iran, ceasefire collapse on Israel-Hezbollah front, proxy coordination in Red Sea, and Iranian nuclear program static at 60% U-235 with 1-2 week breakout window.
